page: Google Cloud Platform c4d-highmem-96 is an x86_64 architecture server instance within the c4d family. It features 96 dedicated virtual central processing units and 756.0 gigabytes of system memory. This configuration yields a memory-to-core ratio of approximately 7.88 gigabytes per virtual central processing unit, positioning it as a high-density memory resource allocation.

This server configuration does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage. Additionally, the instance features zero graphics processing units and does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses. These hardware limitations make the instance less suitable for workloads requiring local high-speed scratch space or direct hardware acceleration.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c4d-highmem-96 offers high memory density per dedicated virtual central processing unit, which is advantageous for memory-bound workloads that do not require local disk input-output or graphics processing unit acceleration. The dedicated processor allocation ensures consistent compute performance. This profile is appropriate for large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise resource planning systems, large-scale data caching, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4d-highmem-96 is an x86_64 architecture server instance in the c4d family, configured with 96 dedicated vCPUs and 756.0 GB of system memory. This configuration provides a high memory-to-core ratio of 7.88 GB per vCPU, designed for memory-intensive workloads. The instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, requiring reliance on network-attached storage for data persistence. It also lacks complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Qualitatively, the server offers high resource density for memory-bound applications, making it suitable for in-memory databases, large-scale caching layers, and enterprise applications that require substantial memory capacity without the need for local physical disks or hardware-accelerated graphics processing.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4d-highmem-96 is an x86_64 server with 96 dedicated vCPUs and 756 GB RAM. It lacks local storage and GPUs, making it suitable for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4d-highmem-96 features 96 dedicated vCPUs and 756 GB RAM, optimized for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
tagline: An x86_64 server configuration featuring ninety-six dedicated vCPUs and seven hundred fifty-six gigabytes of memory for memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 756 GB of system memory providing 7.88 GB per core
No local storage or integrated GPUs included
Designed for in-memory databases and large-scale caching
